examples: The territory ruled by a duke or duchess; a dukedom.
And within a dukedom, the struggle for power frequently occurred.
The old man said, "the duke of Chen is trusted by the king and his dukedom has good relationship with ours."
Twenty-two years before becoming King in 1483, Richard was granted the dukedom of Gloucester.
This is, after all, a modern government, not a medieval dukedom.
